text: Continuous Integration is a software development practice where developers regularly merge their code changes into a central repository. The key element is the CI pipeline, which automatically builds the project and runs a suite of tests every time new code is added. This process helps teams catch issues early, before they become major problems. For example, if a developer introduces a bug, the automated tests will fail immediately, notifying the team. By running these checks constantly, CI ensures that the main codebase remains stable and that problems are fixed as soon as they appear, saving significant time and effort.
